name: feat-ESE-0012-06
title: Multi-pass GPU physics: broadphase + narrowphase + integration
description:
Implement multi-pass GPU physics: broadphase collision detection via spatial hashing, narrowphase sphere-sphere intersection, and Verlet integration with configurable substeps. All three passes execute in a single command encoder submission with no CPU round-trips between passes.

Broadphase: uniform grid spatial hash with atomic insertion (Unity GPU Physics pattern). Narrowphase: 27-neighbor cell search with sphere-sphere tests. Integration: Verlet with N substeps in a GPU loop.

References: GPU Gems Ch.32, Unity GPU Physics (7-kernel pipeline), PSCC paper.
acceptanceCriteria:
- RigidBody and Collider (sphere) components defined
- Broadphase kernel: spatial hash grid with atomic compare-exchange insertion, 4 entities per cell
- Narrowphase kernel: 27-neighbor search, sphere-sphere overlap, impulse response writes to Velocity
- Integration kernel: Verlet integration with configurable substep count (uniform)
- All 3 passes dispatch in single command encoder (clear, populate, detect, integrate)
- GPU physics writes to shared Transform; CPU rendering reads after readback
- 500+ rigid bodies colliding at stable framerate
- Collision pairs match brute-force CPU reference
demoDeliverable: Browser demo: 500+ bouncing spheres in a box. Spheres spawn from top, fall under gravity, collide with each other and walls, settle into piles. Toggle substep count (1, 4, 8) via UI to show stability improvement. Console logs collision pair count per frame and GPU dispatch time. Debug overlay shows spatial hash grid occupancy.
testingScenarios:
- Broadphase: 2 overlapping spheres in same grid cell detected as collision pair
- Broadphase: 2 spheres in distant cells produce no collision pair
- Narrowphase: sphere-sphere impulse response conserves momentum within epsilon
- Integration: single sphere under gravity follows expected parabolic trajectory
- Substeps: 8 substeps produces more stable stacking than 1 substep
- Grid capacity: >4 entities in one cell handled gracefully
- Readback: CPU-side Transform matches GPU-computed positions after physics pass
stakeholderUnderstanding: Multi-pass physics proves the system handles real workloads — multiple compute passes in a single GPU submission with no CPU round-trips between them. The broadphase spatial hash + narrowphase collision + Verlet integration pipeline is the pattern for all future GPU physics. Configurable substeps show how GPU loops avoid the CPU bottleneck of per-substep dispatch. This is the payoff ticket — if this works, the engine has production-grade GPU physics.
